examples.code:
# collections.namedtuple for self-documenting data structures
from collections import namedtuple
Point = namedtuple('Point', ['x', 'y'])
p = Point(x=10, y=20)
print(f'X={p.x}, Y={p.y}')  # Access by field name or index p[0]
examples.explanation: namedtuple provides tuple memory efficiency + attribute name access.
